items.body:
🔴 **Tool Issue** — `package.json:95` (`apiRefLink`)

The `apiRefLink` still contains `?view=azure-node-preview`, but this package is now being released as a stable version (`1.0.0`). The stable API reference should not use the preview view parameter.

**Fix:** Update the link to remove `?view=azure-node-preview`:
```json
"apiRefLink": "(learn.microsoft.com/redacted)
```
Also please report this in the [generation tool repository](https://github.com/Azure/autorest.typescript/issues) if the tooling failed to update this automatically during the beta-to-stable promotion.

🔴 **Design Concern** — `review/arm-planetarycomputer-node.api.md:20–24` (`AzureClouds` enum values)

The `AzureClouds` enum uses `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` for its member names (`AZURE_CHINA_CLOUD`, `AZURE_PUBLIC_CLOUD`, `AZURE_US_GOVERNMENT`). This diverges from the established Azure SDK TypeScript convention where enum member names use **PascalCase** (as seen in `KnownCatalogTier.Basic`, `KnownManagedServiceIdentityType.SystemAssigned`, etc. in this same package).

**Fix:** Rename the enum members to PascalCase in the TypeSpec spec, using `@clientName`, and regenerate:
```
AzureClouds.AzureChinaCloud
AzureClouds.AzurePublicCloud
AzureClouds.AzureUsGovernment
```
Prepare a PR to the spec repo to apply `@clientName` decorators, then trigger SDK regeneration.

🔴 **Design Concern** — `review/arm-planetarycomputer-node.api.md:159` (`KnownVersions._20260415`)

The enum member name `_20260415` starts with an underscore (`_`), which the guidelines flag as poor naming: "Avoid `_` prefixes…this indicates poor naming on the spec side".

**Fix:** Use `@clientName` in the TypeSpec spec to provide a proper name (e.g., `V20260415`) and regenerate the SDK:
```diff
- _20260415 = "2026-04-15"
+ V20260415 = "2026-04-15"
```
Prepare a PR to the spec repo to add a `@clientName` decorator on this enum value, then trigger SDK regeneration.

This PR promotes `@azure/arm-planetarycomputer` from `1.0.0-beta.1` to the first stable release `1.0.0`. The overall structure looks good. Three issues were found: one tooling issue and two naming/convention issues in the public API surface.

<details>
<summary>📊 Structured Report</summary>

```json
{"agent":"mgmt-reviewer","pr":37830,"summary":"issues_found","findings":[{"file":"sdk/planetarycomputer/arm-planetarycomputer/package.json","line":95,"issueType":"tool","category":"apiRefLink","description":"apiRefLink still uses ?view=azure-node-preview for a stable 1.0.0 release; should remove the preview view parameter"},{"file":"sdk/planetarycomputer/arm-planetarycomputer/review/arm-planetarycomputer-node.api.md","line":20,"issueType":"design","category":"naming","description":"AzureClouds enum members use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE instead of PascalCase, diverging from Azure SDK TypeScript naming conventions"},{"file":"sdk/planetarycomputer/arm-planetarycomputer/review/arm-planetarycomputer-node.api.md","line":159,"issueType":"design","category":"naming","description":"KnownVersions enum member _20260415 starts with underscore; should use `@clientName` to produce a PascalCase name like V20260415"}]}
```

</details>
